name: orientation
description: Use this tool to get oriented with the Prefect MCP server.

name: get_identity
description: Get identity and connection information for the current Prefect instance. Returns API URL, type (cloud/oss), and user information if available. Essential for understanding which Prefect instance you're connected to.

name: get_dashboard
description: Get a high-level dashboard overview of the Prefect instance. Returns current flow run statistics, work pool status, and all active concurrency limits (global/tag-based, deployment, work pool, and work queue). Essential for diagnosing flow run delays and bottlenecks.

name: get_deployments
description: Get deployments with optional filters. Returns compact summaries by default. Filter by specific ID(s) for full detail including parameters, parameter_openapi_schema, job_variables, work_pool details, and recent_runs.

filter.description: JSON filter object for advanced querying. Supports all Prefect DeploymentFilter fields.

limit.description: Maximum number of deployments to return

name: get_flows
description: Get flows with optional filters. Returns a list of flows registered in the workspace.

filter.description: JSON filter object for advanced querying. Supports all Prefect FlowFilter fields.

limit.description: Maximum number of flows to return

name: get_flow_runs
description: Get flow runs with optional filters. Returns compact summaries by default. Filter by specific ID(s) for full detail including parameters, inlined deployment info, and work pool info.

filter.description: JSON filter object for advanced querying. Supports all Prefect FlowRunFilter fields.

limit.description: Maximum number of flow runs to return

name: get_flow_run_logs
description: Get execution logs for a flow run. Retrieves log entries from the flow run execution, including timestamps, log levels, and messages.

flow_run_id.description: UUID of the flow run to get logs for

limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return

name: get_task_runs
description: Get task runs with optional filters. Returns a list of task runs and their details matching the filters. Note that 'task_inputs' contains dependency tracking information (upstream task relationships), not the actual parameter values passed to the task.

filter.description: JSON filter object for advanced querying. Supports all Prefect TaskRunFilter fields.

limit.description: Maximum number of task runs to return

name: get_work_pools
description: Get work pools with optional filters. Returns compact summaries by default (name, type, status, concurrency_limit). Filter by specific ID(s) for full detail including work queues, active worker counts, and descriptions. Essential for debugging deployment issues related to flow runs being stuck or not starting.

filter.description: JSON filter object for advanced querying. Supports all Prefect WorkPoolFilter fields.

limit.description: Maximum number of work pools to return

name: read_events
description: Read and filter events from the Prefect instance. Provides a structured view of events with filtering capabilities. When no time range is specified, events from the last 1 hour are returned by default.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return

occurred_after.description: ISO 8601 timestamp to filter events after

occurred_before.description: ISO 8601 timestamp to filter events before

name: get_automations
description: Get automations with optional filters. Returns compact summaries by default (trigger_type, action_count). Filter by specific ID(s) for full detail including trigger config, actions, actions_on_trigger, and actions_on_resolve.

filter.description: JSON filter object for querying automations

limit.description: Maximum number of automations to return

name: get_object_schema
description: Get a schema for an object type.

object_type.description: Name of the object type to get a schema for

name: review_rate_limits
description: Review rate limit usage for this Prefect Cloud account (Cloud only). Prefect Cloud API access is governed by rate limits in different categories. When you see HTTP 429 errors or 'Too Many Requests' responses from the Prefect API, use this tool to identify which operation groups were throttled and when.

since.description: Start time for usage data (ISO 8601). Defaults to 3 days ago.

until.description: End time for usage data (ISO 8601). Defaults to 1 minute ago.

name: docs_search_prefect
description: Search the Prefect knowledgebase for documentation on concepts, usage examples, and best practices.

query.description: a search query to find relevant document excerpts from Prefect's knowledgebase

top_k.description: How many document excerpts to return.
